name: Tokyo Earthquake M7.3 — Supply Chain Shock
description: Magnitude 7.3 earthquake strikes the Tokyo metropolitan region during peak trade hours. Port of Tokyo and Yokohama sustain partial structural damage. Global automotive, semiconductor, and electronics supply chains disrupted. Asia-Pacific freight rates spike 240% within 48 hours.
globalRelevance: Japan supplies 38% of global automotive components and 22% of industrial robotics. Port of Yokohama alone handles 800,000 TEU/month. Disruption cascades into Indian auto sector within 10 days via knock-on parts shortage.
geopoliticalContext: Japan's seismic risk is well-modelled but scale of a Tokyo-area event exceeds most simulation scenarios. India has bilateral trade agreements and emergency logistics cooperation protocols with Japan.

name: California Wildfire — Pacific Port Disruption
description: Extreme wildfire event driven by Santa Ana wind conditions destroys power infrastructure serving the Port of Los Angeles and Long Beach — the busiest port complex in the Western Hemisphere. Smoke, road closures, and power outages halt container operations for 72 hours. Indian pharmaceutical and IT hardware imports via the Pacific face 3-week backlogs.
globalRelevance: LA / Long Beach handles 40% of US container imports. Indian pharma and electronics exports to the US are significantly impacted. Alternative routing via East Coast adds 18 days transit time.
geopoliticalContext: Climate-driven wildfire risk in California is increasing annually. India-US bilateral trade in 2023 exceeded $190B. A Pacific port disruption directly affects Indian export timelines.

name: Rotterdam Port Disruption
description: Extreme low Rhine river water levels combined with dock worker strike cause a 96-hour near-total halt at the Port of Rotterdam — Europe's largest port. 42 nations dependent on Rotterdam for container transshipment face cascading import/export delays. Inland barge network severely constrained.
globalRelevance: Rotterdam handles 14.5 million TEU/year serving 42+ nations. India's textile and chemical exports to Europe transship through Rotterdam. Disruption shifts cargo to Antwerp and Hamburg at premium freight rates.
geopoliticalContext: Europe's gateway port is increasingly at risk from climate-driven Rhine water level drops. India-EU FTA negotiations make European port reliability strategically important for Indian export planning.

name: Red Sea Trade Corridor Crisis
description: Ongoing Houthi attacks on commercial shipping in the Red Sea force 85% of container traffic to divert around the Cape of Good Hope, adding 10–14 days to Europe-Asia transit. Insurance surcharges, fuel costs, and vessel diversions push freight rates to 2021 pandemic-era highs. Indian export competitiveness severely impacted.
globalRelevance: 12% of global trade transits the Red Sea / Suez Canal route. Indian textile, pharmaceutical, and IT exports to Europe add 14 days via Cape routing. Energy imports and fertilizer supply chains directly affected, raising food inflation risk.
geopoliticalContext: Red Sea crisis (2023–2024) is an active real-world scenario. Indian Navy deployed to escort commercial vessels. Direct link between geopolitical instability and economic resilience planning.
